publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-05-09 23:54:08 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Letters received by Horace L. Edgar Pratt, a Protestant Episcopal rector on Staten Island, New York; also, a Civil War draft exemption certificate issued to John A Kernochan, Sept. 10, 1863; Queens County documents including a four year indenture apprenticing Joseph Lawrence to Effingham Lawrence; a bill of sale selling Joseph, a Negro boy; a receipt for a twelve pound bounty for William Aitken for New York militia service during the Revolutionary War; and a proclamation of Governor George Clinton.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Letters received by Horace L. Edgar Pratt, a Protestant Episcopal rector on Staten Island, New York; also, a Civil War draft exemption certificate issued to John A Kernochan, Sept. 10, 1863; Queens County documents including a four year indenture apprenticing Joseph Lawrence to Effingham Lawrence; a bill of sale selling Joseph, a Negro boy; a receipt for a twelve pound bounty for William Aitken for New York militia service during the Revolutionary War; and a proclamation of Governor George Clinton.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Pratt family papers, #223. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: Staten Island (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.
controlaccess.subject: Slavery -- New York (State) -- Queens County.
controlaccess.corpname: United States. Army -- : Recruiting, enlistment, etc. -- : Civil War, 1861-1865

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: "My Dear Sir" - Rev. H. E. Pratt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Certificate of Non-Liability to be Given by the Board of Enrollment for John A. Kernochan to be given by the Board of Enrollment for John A. Kernochan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Rev. H. A. E. Pratt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Rev, Horace Pratt, Staten Island

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From: Signed by J. G. Eaton, Lieut.
Re: Permit to enter Boston Navy Yard for day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Invitation to Commemorative Reception to be given for Rt. Rev. Horatio Potter, Bishop of New York (no addressee) (printed)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Rev. Horace Pratt, 137 E. 45th St.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Invitation and menu for the 94th Anniversary of the St. George's Society of New York at Delmonico's

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Piece of paper with: . . ,"Richard Hoffman, Jany. (?) 24th 83, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Invitation from the Trustees of the Metropolitan Museum of Art to attend a lecture by Prof. Charles Eliot Norton (printed) (no addressee)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Kev. Horace L. Edgar Pratt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From: Wm. W. Jones, Deputy Collector
Re: Custom House, New York, pass to Assistant Appraisers Office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Announcement of "Piano-Lecture-Concerts" of Jerome Hopkins, New York City, 1st Appearances since his European successes...

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Rev. H. L. E. Pratt
From: Louis Fitzgerald, Treasurer, Corporation of the Church of the Epiphany, Mercantile Trust Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From: C. A. Dana, The Sun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From: Alan S. Webb, The College of the City of New York, President's Office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From: J. H. Stoddart (perhaps the actor, James Henry, 1827-1907)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Slip of paper; "House of Rep's, U. S. Public Document. Free. M. C." (printed). "S. S. Cox, John S. Leng, Esqr. , Iron Merchant, No 4 Fletcher St., New York City, N.Y/ (written).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Slip of paper: "J. Hyatt Smith M. C."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Fragment of paper, "Long" and "S S Col M. C."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Fragment of paper, "House of Representatives U. S. Part of Cong. Record. Free. L. K. Pratt, 1017 Lex. Av., NY, R (illegible), M. C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To: Slip of paper: "Votre tout Dévané: Cte de Lippe-Weisenfeld"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional information on certain correspondents and their letters or other papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lawyer, author. Letter of introduction to Dallas B. Pratt, November 8, 1889

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Governor of New York. Proclamation, August 20, 1784, in own handwriting, convening Assembly.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed letter to Lawrence K. Pratt, October 27, 1891, sending Pratt two tickets to a business men's meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Journalist, lecturer, author. Letter to Rev. Horace Pratt, November 21, 1879, referring to election of 1879. "The result of the election seems to me very valuable as showing that there is independence enough to hold machines in check."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Editor, New York Sun. Letter to Dallas B. Pratt, June 3, 1893, introducing Gerrit Smith Glen, who was organizing "The National Continental League" for the political union of the United States and Canada by "honorable and peaceful means."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mrs. Jefferson Davis. Letter to Mrs. Dallas Bache Pratt asking that daughter be excused from dinner party because of illness. February 17, 1893.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prelate of Catholic Church in later life, having left the Protestant Episcopal Church in 1855. Letter, May 3, 1881, saying he can't attend the wedding of his godson and Amy Pratt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed letter, November 1891, thanking an unidentified person for congratulations on Flower's election as Governor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composer, pianist, lecturer. Printed bill, advertising lecture-concert, May 5, 1892. Letter to C.W. Leng, September 16, 1892.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Certificate of non-liability to be given by the board of enrollment." John A Kernochan of New York City has given "satisfactory evidence that he is not properly subject to do military duty. . ." September 10, 1863. Letter to James B. Pratt from J. Frederick Kernochan concerning estate of J.A.K., May 28, 1891.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A founder of the New York World. Letter, August 10, 1867, replying to Rev. H.L.E. Pratt, saying that the World would be pleased for an "occasional letter" during Pratt's projected southern trip.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Protestant Episcopal clergyman, Secretary to Bishop of New York (1886-1908), Canon of St. John the Divine after 1911. Letter of introduction to Dallas Bache Pratt, January 22, 1903.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Protestant Episcopal clergyman, author. All Souls Church, New York City. Thank-you note, January 2, 1877, to Rev. Pratt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter, April 7, 1869, requesting Rev. Horace L. Edgar Pratt to preach one Sunday at Grace Church, Brooklyn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bishop, Protestant Episcopal Church, Louisiana. Founder, University of the South, Sewanee, Tennessee, 1860. Letter, December 8, 1853 to Rev. H.L.E Pratt concerning a Post for Pratt in the south. Discussion of salaries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nephew of Horatio, Bishop of New York after 1887. Letter, March 31, 1839, to warden of Church of Holy Sepulchre.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bishop of New York. Letter, August 24, 1871, to Rev. Pratt, about a forthcoming visit of Potter to the Staten Island church. Printed invitation to a reception, November 25, 1879, celebrating 25 years of Potter's Episcopate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter, February 19, 1889, concerning affairs of the Church of the Holy Sepulchre.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rector, Church of the Holy Trinity, New York. letter to a Mrs. Good, May 3, 1894, thanking her for a gift for "the summer home."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President, City College of New York. Note, April 17, 1895 to unknown person.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bishop, Florida. Letter to Rev. Pratt, December 10, 1873, about possible post in Florida.
